title: Finite Polar Decomposition Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite polar bad positive-factor diagonal QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact polar replay reads P[1,1] as 5 while a malformed row claims 4, isolating a compact scalar equality conflict for the Farkas route.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional polar rows only when they add distinct partial-isometry, rank-deficient, square-root, perturbation, residual-bound, or iterative-algorithm pressure; keep polar existence, uniqueness, functional calculus, and floating-point stability in theorem or numerical-honesty lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed rational A, U, U^T, P, A^T*A, P^2, positive diagonal entries, leading minors, trace, determinant, and product rows as deterministic exact-rational replay obligations.
Route the false positive-factor diagonal row through a source-linked QF_LRA artifact and checked Farkas evidence after exact replay reads the conflicting diagonal entry.
Expose the pack through the matrix computation learner and consumer query surfaces.

Keep polar decomposition existence, uniqueness, partial-isometry theory, square-root functional calculus, iterative algorithms, perturbation theory, and floating-point stability claims marked Lean or numerical-honesty horizon.
